label: Creator Analytics Dashboard — NPS demand spike
description: 68 NPS survey responses in Q1 explicitly requesting a creator-facing analytics dashboard. Volume increased 40% over 6 weeks. Top request: impressions breakdown by content type and time-of-day performance.

label: Creator retention pain — lack of performance visibility
description: 14 creator interviews surfaced a consistent theme: creators can't see what's working. They churn to Substack and Beehiiv where dashboards exist. Quote: 'I'm flying blind. I post and hope.'

label: Post scheduling UX — friction in publish workflow
description: 32 NPS requests asking for scheduled posts and a content calendar view. Current workflow requires manual posting, causing creators to miss optimal publish windows.

label: Support ticket cluster — analytics data not visible
description: Support ticket cluster (41 tickets, March) reporting that creators cannot see post performance metrics from the creator dashboard. Engineering confirmed UI gap — data exists in backend but is not surfaced.

label: Creator monetization interest — interview signal
description: 9 creator interviews showed high interest in newsletter monetization and paid subscriptions. Creators currently monetizing via external tools (Gumroad, Patreon) due to lack of native options.

label: Exit survey cluster — analytics top churn reason
description: Q1 exit survey analysis across 312 churned creators. 34% cited 'no analytics' as primary reason for leaving. 21% mentioned Substack's dashboard as the pull factor. Highest exit signal in 6 quarters.

label: Creator monetization workflow — high friction signal
description: 7 creator interviews noted the friction of using external tools (Gumroad, Patreon) alongside LinkedIn. Quote: 'I have three dashboards open at once. LinkedIn sends the audience, I collect money elsewhere. That's broken.'

label: Q2 Planning: Prioritize Post Scheduling v2
description: Q2 planning session decided to ship Post Scheduling v2 as the primary creator tools investment. Rationale: scheduling was the highest-volume NPS request at the time and lowest engineering complexity.

metadata.decisionMakers: Alex Chen, VP Product, Eng Lead

label: Executive Priority Shift: Newsletter Monetization to Q3
description: VP of Revenue mandated Newsletter Monetization as a Q3 revenue initiative after Substack reported $180M ARR milestone. Monetization moved from Q4 to Q3, creating tension with Creator Analytics.

label: Q1 Deprioritization: Creator Fund Beta pushed to H2
description: Creator Fund Beta was deprioritized in Q1 planning due to legal complexity around payment processing and limited engineering capacity. Moved to H2 2026 pending compliance review.

label: Q3 Prioritization Analysis: Analytics vs Monetization
description: Ongoing strategic tension between Creator Analytics Dashboard (highest user demand, 68 NPS requests, 40% growth) and Newsletter Monetization (executive mandate, revenue signal). Mira's recommendation: Analytics first — user retention risk is acute.

label: Creator Fund legal hold — payment processor exclusion
description: Legal team issued a hold on Creator Fund Beta after determining that LinkedIn's primary payment processor (Stripe) cannot support the creator fund model due to regulatory classification issues in 7 markets.

label: Creator Analytics Dashboard
description: Self-serve analytics dashboard for creators showing impressions, reach, engagement rate, follower growth, and content performance breakdowns. Parity with Substack's creator analytics.

label: Post Scheduling v2
description: Content scheduling with calendar view, optimal time recommendations, and bulk scheduling. Supports LinkedIn Articles, newsletters, and short-form posts.

label: Newsletter Monetization
description: Native paid newsletter subscriptions, reader payment processing, creator payout dashboard, and subscription tier management. Direct competition to Substack's core revenue model.

label: Creator Fund Beta
description: LinkedIn-sponsored creator fund distributing $5M to qualifying creators based on engagement metrics. Requires payment processing integration and compliance review.

label: Content Performance Benchmarking
description: Sub-feature of Creator Analytics: benchmark a creator's content performance against anonymized peer averages by category, follower tier, and post type. Answers 'Am I growing fast enough?'

label: Creator Content Calendar
description: Visual calendar view for scheduling posts, newsletters, and articles. Drag-and-drop rescheduling, optimal posting time overlay (ML-powered), and integration with LinkedIn drafts.

label: Weekly Active Creators (WAC)
description: Number of unique creators who publish at least one piece of content per week. Current: 2.3M. Target: 3.1M by end of Q3. Leading indicator for creator platform health.

label: Post Completion Rate
description: Percentage of started posts that are published (not abandoned). Current: 61%. Goal: 75%. Low completion rate correlates with scheduling friction and lack of draft management.

label: Creator Revenue per User
description: Average revenue earned per monetized creator per month. Currently $0 on LinkedIn (no native monetization). Benchmark: Substack creators earn avg $340/month. This is the key gap metric.

label: Follower Growth Rate (FGR)
description: Month-over-month follower growth for creators with 10K+ followers. Current: declining 8% YoY among Power Creators. Industry benchmark (Substack): +12% MoM for newsletter subscribers. Critical health indicator.

label: Power Creator Churn Rate
description: Monthly churn rate for Power Creators (50K+ followers). Q1 2026: 3x higher than Q4 2025. 34% of churned creators cited analytics gap in exit surveys. Direct input into Q3 prioritization.

label: Power Creator
description: Professional content creator with 10K+ followers. Posts 5+ times/week across articles, posts, and newsletters. Primary pain: no analytics visibility, can't optimize content strategy. High churn risk to Substack.

label: Casual User
description: LinkedIn member who occasionally shares posts and articles. Posts 1-2 times/month. Does not identify as a creator. Primary need: professional networking, not content creation.

label: Newsletter Subscriber
description: Reader who follows and pays for creator newsletters. Typically a professional who values curated expert content. Key persona for Newsletter Monetization — their willingness to pay determines creator revenue potential.

label: Substack
description: Newsletter platform with 35M active readers. Launched creator analytics dashboard in April 2026 — showing impressions, open rates, subscriber growth charts. Creator-first positioning is accelerating LinkedIn creator churn.

label: Beehiiv
description: Newsletter and creator growth platform. Strong analytics suite — cohort retention, referral tracking, engagement heatmaps. Growing 20% MoM. Targeting LinkedIn's power creators with migration tools.

label: TikTok Creator Tools
description: TikTok Creator Studio offers real-time analytics, revenue dashboard, and Creator Fund. Strong in video content. Less relevant for LinkedIn's professional audience but relevant for creator monetization benchmarking.

label: Ghost.org
description: Open-source publishing platform with strong native monetization (memberships, paid tiers). Growing adoption among professional writers migrating from Substack. Offers clean analytics + Stripe-native payouts.

label: Epic: Creator Analytics Dashboard
description: Q3 epic to ship a creator-facing analytics dashboard. Scope: impressions, reach, engagement rate, follower growth charts, content performance breakdown by type, and export capabilities.

label: Epic: Post Scheduling v2
description: Q2 epic shipping now. Calendar view, optimal time recommendations (ML-powered), bulk scheduling, and draft management. Target GA: May 30, 2026.

label: Epic: Newsletter Monetization
description: Q3/Q4 epic for native newsletter paid subscriptions. Includes payment processing, creator payout dashboard, subscriber management, and pricing tiers. Competing priority with Creator Analytics.

label: Epic: Creator Fund Beta
description: H2 2026 epic (deprioritized from Q1). LinkedIn-funded creator rewards program. Currently blocked on legal review of payment processing classification.

label: Epic: Creator Growth Acceleration
description: Cross-functional H2 initiative targeting Follower Growth Rate recovery for Power Creators. Combines analytics visibility, content recommendations, and network amplification to reverse 8% YoY FGR decline.

label: Exec note: Q3 must show revenue signal
description: CEO note from all-hands (March 3): 'Creator Tools needs to show a revenue signal in Q3 or we risk budget cuts in the next planning cycle. Monetization is not optional.' Directly influences Q3 prioritization.

label: Creator team sync: Analytics churn signal confirmed
description: April 8 creator team weekly sync. Growth team confirmed: creators with 50K+ followers showing 3x higher churn in Q1 vs Q4. Exit survey data: 34% cited 'lack of analytics' as primary reason. Alex flagged for Q3 planning.

metadata.attendees: Alex Chen, Growth Lead, Data PM

label: Research: Follower Growth Rate declining for power creators
description: Internal data team analysis: Follower Growth Rate for creators with 10K+ followers declined 8% YoY. Correlated with Substack and Beehiiv launch activity. Analytics visibility may be key retention lever.

label: Legal memo: payment processing classification issue
description: Legal team memo (Dec 10): Stripe cannot support Creator Fund model in 7 markets due to regulatory classification of creator fund payments as 'employment income' rather than 'platform incentive.' Blocks entire Creator Fund design.

label: Growth report: Power Creator cohort analysis Q1 2026
description: Growth team Q1 cohort analysis: Power Creators who posted 5+ times/week retained at 91%. Those who posted 1-2 times/week retained at 67%. Conclusion: creation frequency is the strongest retention predictor, and analytics visibility is its primary enabler.

label: Engineering spike: analytics data pipeline availability
description: Engineering spike confirmed: all required analytics data (impressions, reach, engagement, follower delta) is already computed in the data warehouse and available via internal API. Zero pipeline work needed — analytics is a pure frontend build.
